commands.author: Noquetoo

target.title: [Bug]: Incomplete turn / stream cut mid-tool-calls with NVIDIA Build provider (GLM 5.1, MiniMax M2.7)
target.body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

With NVIDIA Build provider, agent stream cuts silently mid-execution during tool calls — stopReason=stop with tools=3 pending, no user notification, session enters zombie state.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure NVIDIA Build (https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1) as provider with model z-ai/glm-5.1 or minimaxai/minimax-m2.7.
2. Send a task via Telegram requiring multiple tool calls (e.g. "review this codebase and fix these bugs").
3. Agent starts working — Telegram shows typing indicator.
4. After variable time (seconds to minutes), typing indicator disappears.
5. No response arrives and no error is sent to the user.

### Expected behavior

Agent completes tool calls and delivers a response, or sends an explicit error message to the user if the stream fails. Session should abort cleanly instead of entering a zombie retry loop.

### Actual behavior

Stream closes with stopReason=stop while 3 tool calls are pending (payloads=0 tools=3, replaySafe=no). User receives no notification. Session enters zombie state retrying every 30 minutes via heartbeat indefinitely. Gateway logs show: "incomplete turn detected — stopReason=stop payloads=0 tools=3 replaySafe=no" and "candidate_failed reason=format code=incomplete_result fallbackStepFinalOutcome=chain_exhausted".

### OpenClaw version

2026.6.9 (c645ec4)

### Operating system

Ubuntu 24.04, arm64

### Install method

npm global

### Model

nvidia/z-ai/glm-5.1, nvidia/minimaxai/minimax-m2.7

### Provider / routing chain

openclaw -> nvidia (https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1)

### Additional provider/model setup details

Models defined in plugin catalog at extensions/nvidia/openclaw.plugin.json. No fallback configured for these models. NVIDIA-only setup, no secondary provider available. Models selected at runtime via /models Telegram command.

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
WARN agent/embedded: incomplete turn detected
  provider=nvidia/z-ai/glm-5.1
  stopReason=stop
  payloads=0 tools=3
  replaySafe=no
  compactions=0
  emptyRetries=0/1

ERROR diagnostic: lane task error
  error="FailoverError: 410 status code (no body)"

WARN compaction: Full summarization failed: Summarization failed: 410 status code (no body)
  (repeated 8 times consecutively)

WARN model-fallback/decision: candidate_failed
  reason=format
  code=incomplete_result
  fallbackStepFinalOutcome=chain_exhausted

Stability: dropped=1966 events
Memory at time of issue: rss=615 MiB heap=332 MiB
```

### Impact and severity

Affected: Single-user Telegram setup on NVIDIA Build API (arm64 VPS).
Severity: High — blocks all multi-tool agentic tasks completely.
Frequency: Always reproducible with code audit or multi-file review tasks.
Consequence: Agent silently fails every tool-heavy task. User must manually detect failure and restart session. Zombie sessions accumulate consuming gateway resources for hours (default stuckSessionAbortMs was 14400000ms).

### Additional information

- parallel_tool_calls cannot be set to false via openclaw.json — not exposed in agent or model schema. This would be the natural workaround for parallel tool call stream cuts.
- compaction also fails with 410 when NVIDIA is the only provider, creating a secondary failure path.
- loopDetection was disabled by default in generated config.
- stuckSessionAbortMs defaults to 14400000ms (4 hours), allowing zombie sessions to persist undetected.
- No regression baseline available — NVIDIA Build has been the only provider from initial setup.
target.author: Noquetoo
